repo_prior_art.cite: doc/API.md §3.1 Manski bounded-outcome entry plus eid_proximal_phase_v1 sharpness-condition discussion; no same proximal bridge-realization frontier

published_prior_art.cite: BalkePearl1997 gives finite LP sharp bounds; GhassamiShpitserTchetgen2023 gives proxy partial-ID bounds, but neither states this proximal bridge-endpoint Farkas test

repo_prior_art.cite: eid_proximal_phase_v1 conjectured universal finite realization in the opposite direction; this asks for open-class nonrealization

published_prior_art.cite: ZhangLiMiaoTchetgen2023 and GhassamiShpitserTchetgen2023 cover nonunique bridges and proxy partial ID, not open-class finite latent nonrealizability of bridge endpoints

repo_prior_art.cite: no in-catalogue CausalSmith proposal gives a concrete binary proximal bridge nonrealization witness

published_prior_art.cite: MiaoGengTchetgen2018, CuiPuShiMiaoTchetgen2024, and BalkePearl1997 do not provide this proximal binary support-gap witness

structure_flags.label: Introduction
structure_flags.one_line: §4 has only five required subparagraphs; it is missing a separate 'how research benefits from the conclusion' paragraph.

structure_flags.label: Bibliography
structure_flags.one_line: ZhangLiMiaoTchetgen2023 is unstable/misdated: the verified SPL article is 2023, volume 198, 109836, while 2024 110055 is a corrigendum, not the listed volume 204 article.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 2
novelty_flags.one_line: The §8 witness claim cites MiaoGengTchetgen2018, CuiPuShiMiaoTchetgen2024, and BalkePearl1997 without section/equation/page anchors tying them to the specific witness gap.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 2
novelty_flags.one_line: The downstream consumer is quoted as a prior 'pid_mediation_proxy reviewer warning', not a named published workflow, applied domain, or open problem.

soundness_flags.label: Theorem 1
soundness_flags.one_line: Delta_BR(P)=0 is equivalent to envelope equality only if Theta_lat(P) is known to be contained in Theta_br(P) or the latent envelope is defined through realized bridge pairs; that inclusion is not stated.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 1
soundness_flags.one_line: The claimed 'open set' of rank-one binary-proxy laws is not open in the ambient observable-law simplex; it must be stated as relative openness in a fixed rank-one stratum.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 2
soundness_flags.one_line: The concrete binary witness appears latently realizable: take U=W, Z independent, E[Y(1)|U]=U, and E[Y(0)|U]=1/4, which matches the displayed bridge endpoint instead of violating Farkas feasibility.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 2
soundness_flags.one_line: Example 1 specifies P(A), P(Z,W|A), and conditional means m_a(z), but not the full finite observable law P(Y,A,W,Z,X) required by the locked setup and response-type LP.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 1 | Conjecture 2
soundness_flags.one_line: Assumption witness-margin assumes a positive bridge-realization support gap for the Section 9 witness, which is the load-bearing conclusion Conjecture 2 is supposed to establish.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Run the finite response-type feasibility LP for the displayed binary law and either exhibit the dual certificate or replace the witness with a fully specified observable law whose endpoint is actually infeasible.
Redefine Theta_lat(P) as the image of latent completions paired with an explicit h in the bounded bridge fiber, or add a lemma/assumption proving Theta_lat(P) is contained in Theta_br(P).
Move the positive support gap out of Assumption witness-margin and define eta_BR from primitive cell/rank margins plus an independently stated Farkas certificate.
Add location-level anchors for the proximal and LP comparator papers in the Conjecture 2 novelty block, especially the exact bridge-equation and response-type LP statements being extended.

recommended_next_step: Reject this v1; redraft only after a verified nonrealizable finite witness replaces Example 1 and the latent-envelope definition is made noncircular.
